title: Final Fantasy XIV Says No to Butt Sliders — For Now
summary: Do you ever wish you could make your video game character look exactly how you want? Players of Final Fantasy XIV (that's a big online game) have been asking for something called "butt sliders" — a way to change the size of their character's rear end. At the annual Fan Festival in Anaheim, the game's director, Naoki Yoshida (everyone calls him Yoshi-P), showed a slide about new character creation options. He listed things like new hair colors, eye colors, and face paint. But then he said something that made the crowd go wild: "No butt sliders... yet." He said the word "yet" with a little smile, and the audience laughed and cheered. One fan even tweeted a video of the moment with the caption "BUTT SLIDERS CONFIRMED???" So while you can't change your character's butt right now, Square Enix might add it in the future — maybe in the next big expansion called Evercold, coming in January. Cool, right?
why_it_matters: This shows that video game companies pay attention to what players want, and sometimes they tease future features — just like when you ask for a change in your favorite toy or game.

questions.correct_answer: In the next expansion called Evercold in January

background_read: Final Fantasy XIV is a big online game where millions of players adventure together in a fantasy world.
Square Enix is the company that makes the game, and they hold Fan Festivals every year to share news with players.
Naoki Yoshida (called Yoshi-P) is the game's director and is famous for talking directly with fans.
Article_Structure: WHO: Naoki Yoshida (Yoshi-P) and players of Final Fantasy XIV.
WHAT: Yoshi-P surprised fans by saying 'No butt sliders... yet', hinting they might come in the future.
WHERE: At the annual Fan Festival in Anaheim, California.
WHY: Players had been asking for more ways to customize their characters.

perspectives.perspective: Positive for fans who want more customization
perspectives.description: Players who love making their characters look unique are excited that butt sliders might come, giving them even more control.
Some people think asking for butt sliders is silly and that developers should focus on more important game features instead.
The fan reaction shows how much players care about their characters' appearance, which is a big part of many online games.
